800	async fn retention_event_participation(
801	    conn: &DatabaseConnection,
802	    start: &NaiveDate,
803	    end: &NaiveDate,
804	    periods: i32,
805	) -> AppResult<Vec<RetentionRow>> {
806	    // Step 1: Get cohort sizes — first month each participant appeared.
807	    let cohort_sql = "
808	        SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(e.created_at)) AS cohort, COUNT(DISTINCT r.participant_id) AS cohort_size
809	        FROM results r
810	        JOIN events e ON r.event_id = e.id
811	        WHERE date(e.created_at) >= ? AND date(e.created_at) <= ?
812	        GROUP BY cohort
813	        ORDER BY cohort";
814	
815	    let cohort_rows = conn
816	        .query_all(Statement::from_sql_and_values(
817	            DbBackend::Sqlite,
818	            cohort_sql,
819	            vec![start.to_string().into(), end.to_string().into()],
820	        ))
821	        .await
822	        .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
823	
824	    let mut result_rows = Vec::new();
825	
826	    for crow in &cohort_rows {
827	        let cohort: String = crow.try_get("", "cohort").unwrap_or_default();
828	        let cohort_size: i64 = crow.try_get("", "cohort_size").unwrap_or(0);
829	
830	        if cohort.is_empty() || cohort_size == 0 {
831	            continue;
832	        }
833	
834	        let mut period_data = Vec::new();
835	
836	        for p in 1..=periods {
837	            // Count participants from this cohort who also appeared in cohort+p months.
838	            let ret_sql = format!(
839	                "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT r2.participant_id) AS n
840	                 FROM results r1
841	                 JOIN events e1 ON r1.event_id = e1.id
842	                 JOIN results r2 ON r1.participant_id = r2.participant_id
843	                 JOIN events e2 ON r2.event_id = e2.id
844	                 WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(e1.created_at)) = ?
845	                   AND strftime('%Y-%m', e2.created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', date(? || '-01', '+{p} month'))
846	                 GROUP BY r1.participant_id
847	                 HAVING COUNT(*) > 0"
848	            );
849	
850	            // Simpler approach: count participants in that cohort who appeared in the Nth month.
851	            let simple_sql = format!(
852	                "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT r.participant_id) AS n
853	                 FROM results r
854	                 JOIN events e ON r.event_id = e.id
855	                 WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', e.created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', date(? || '-01', '+{p} month'))
856	                   AND r.participant_id IN (
857	                     SELECT DISTINCT r2.participant_id
858	                     FROM results r2
859	                     JOIN events e2 ON r2.event_id = e2.id
860	                     WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', e2.created_at) = ?
861	                   )"
862	            );
863	
864	            let count = query_count(
865	                conn,
866	                &simple_sql,
867	                vec![cohort.clone().into(), cohort.clone().into()],
868	            )
869	            .await
870	            .unwrap_or(0);
871	
872	            let rate = if cohort_size > 0 {
873	                (Decimal::from(count) / Decimal::from(cohort_size))
874	                    .round_dp(4)
875	                    .to_string()
876	            } else {
877	                "0.0000".into()
878	            };
879	
880	            period_data.push(RetentionPeriod {
881	                period: p,
882	                count,
883	                rate,
884	            });
885	        }
886	
887	        result_rows.push(RetentionRow {
888	            cohort,
889	            cohort_size,
890	            periods: period_data,
891	        });
892	    }
893	
894	    Ok(result_rows)
895	}

715	async fn funnel_refund_approval(
716	    conn: &DatabaseConnection,
717	    start: &NaiveDate,
718	    end: &NaiveDate,
719	) -> AppResult<Vec<FunnelStep>> {
720	    let params: Vec<sea_orm::Value> = vec![start.to_string().into(), end.to_string().into()];
721	    let date_filter = "date(created_at) >= ? AND date(created_at) <= ?";
722	
723	    let requested = query_count(
724	        conn,
725	        &format!("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM payment_refunds WHERE {date_filter}"),
726	        params.clone(),
727	    )
728	    .await?;
729	
730	    let escalated = query_count(
731	        conn,
732	        &format!(
733	            "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM payment_refunds \
734	             WHERE status IN ('pending_auditor','approved') AND {date_filter}"
735	        ),
736	        params.clone(),
737	    )
738	    .await?;
739	
740	    let approved = query_count(
741	        conn,
742	        &format!(
743	            "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM payment_refunds WHERE status = 'approved' AND {date_filter}"
744	        ),
745	        params,
746	    )
747	    .await?;
748	
749	    Ok(build_funnel_steps(vec![
750	        ("requested", requested),
751	        ("finance_approved", escalated),
752	        ("fully_approved", approved),
753	    ]))
754	}

760	pub async fn compute_retention(
761	    conn: &DatabaseConnection,
762	    q: RetentionQuery,
763	) -> AppResult<RetentionResponse> {
764	    let start = q
765	        .start_date
766	        .as_deref()
767	        .map(|s| parse_date_param(s, "start_date"))
768	        .transpose()?
769	        .unwrap_or_else(|| NaiveDate::from_ymd_opt(2023, 1, 1).unwrap());
770	
771	    let end = q
772	        .end_date
773	        .as_deref()
774	        .map(|s| parse_date_param(s, "end_date"))
775	        .transpose()?
776	        .unwrap_or_else(|| Utc::now().date_naive());
777	
778	    let periods = q.periods.unwrap_or(3).clamp(1, 6);
779	
780	    let rows = match q.retention_type.as_str() {
781	        "event_participation" => retention_event_participation(conn, &start, &end, periods).await?,
782	        "invoice_payment" => retention_invoice_payment(conn, &start, &end, periods).await?,
783	        other => {
784	            return Err(AppError::BadRequest(format!(
785	                "Unknown retention_type '{other}'. Valid: event_participation, invoice_payment"
786	            )));
787	        }
788	    };
789	
790	    Ok(RetentionResponse {
791	        retention_type: q.retention_type,
792	        start_date: start.to_string(),
793	        end_date: end.to_string(),
794	        rows,
795	    })
796	}

133	/// Build an `AssetResponse`, decrypting `serial_number` if present.
134	fn to_response(cipher: &Cipher, m: &asset_entity::Model) -> AppResult<AssetResponse> {
135	    let (monthly, book_value, fully_dep) = compute_depreciation(
136	        m.procurement_cost,
137	        m.procurement_date.as_deref(),
138	        m.useful_life_months,
139	    );
140	    let cost_f = m
141	        .procurement_cost
142	        .map(|d| d.to_string().parse::<f64>().unwrap_or(0.0));
143	
144	    let serial_number_plain = cipher
145	        .decrypt_opt(m.serial_number.as_deref())
146	        .map_err(dec_err)?;
147	
148	    Ok(AssetResponse {
149	        id: m.id,
150	        asset_code: m.asset_code.clone(),
151	        category: category_str(&m.category).to_owned(),
152	        brand: m.brand.clone(),
153	        model: m.model.clone(),
154	        serial_number: serial_number_plain,
155	        status: status_str(&m.status).to_owned(),
156	        owner_id: m.owner_id,
157	        responsible_person_id: m.responsible_person_id,
158	        procurement_cost: cost_f,
159	        procurement_date: m.procurement_date.clone(),
160	        useful_life_months: m.useful_life_months,
161	        monthly_depreciation: monthly,
162	        current_book_value: book_value,
163	        fully_depreciated: fully_dep,
164	        notes: m.notes.clone(),
165	        created_at: m.created_at.to_rfc3339(),
166	        updated_at: m.updated_at.to_rfc3339(),
167	    })
168	}

170	/// Produce an audit snapshot with `serial_number` redacted.
171	///
172	/// The snapshot is written to the append-only audit log — sensitive identifiers
173	/// must never appear in plaintext in audit trails.
174	fn snapshot_json(m: &asset_entity::Model) -> String {
175	    serde_json::json!({
176	        "id":                    m.id,
177	        "asset_code":            m.asset_code,
178	        "category":              category_str(&m.category),
179	        "brand":                 m.brand,
180	        "model":                 m.model,
181	        "serial_number":         Cipher::mask(),
182	        "status":                status_str(&m.status),
183	        "owner_id":              m.owner_id,
184	        "responsible_person_id": m.responsible_person_id,
185	        "procurement_cost":      m.procurement_cost.map(|d| d.to_string()),
186	        "procurement_date":      m.procurement_date,
187	        "useful_life_months":    m.useful_life_months,
188	        "notes":                 m.notes,
189	        "updated_at":            m.updated_at.to_rfc3339(),
190	    })
191	    .to_string()
192	}

194	async fn record_audit(
195	    conn: &impl sea_orm::ConnectionTrait,
196	    asset_id: i64,
197	    changed_by: i64,
198	    change_type: &str,
199	    snapshot: &str,
200	) -> Result<(), sea_orm::DbErr> {
201	    AuditActiveModel {
202	        asset_id: Set(asset_id),
203	        changed_by: Set(changed_by),
204	        change_type: Set(change_type.to_owned()),
205	        snapshot: Set(snapshot.to_owned()),
206	        changed_at: Set(Utc::now()),
207	        ..Default::default()
208	    }
209	    .insert(conn)
210	    .await?;
211	
212	    // Also write to the unified audit trail for cross-entity queryability.
213	    let snapshot_val: serde_json::Value =
214	        serde_json::from_str(snapshot).unwrap_or(serde_json::Value::Object(Default::default()));
215	    let action = format!("asset.{change_type}");
216	    audit::service::append(
217	        conn,
218	        changed_by,
219	        &action,
220	        "asset",
221	        asset_id,
222	        snapshot_val,
223	        serde_json::json!({}),
224	    )
225	    .await
226	    .map_err(|e| sea_orm::DbErr::Custom(e.to_string()))?;
227	
228	    Ok(())
229	}

13	
14	/// Create a new draft invoice.
15	///
16	/// `invoice_no` must be globally unique.  `tax_rate` is a fraction in [0, 1]
17	/// (e.g. `0.10` for 10 %).  The invoice starts with zero line items; add lines
18	/// before issuing.
19	///
20	/// **Required permission:** `financials:write`
21	#[post("/invoices", data = "<body>")]
22	pub async fn create_invoice(
23	    guard: RequireFinancialsWrite,
24	    body: Json<CreateInvoiceRequest>,
25	    conn: &State<DatabaseConnection>,
26	) -> AppResult<Json<InvoiceResponse>> {
27	    let resp = service::create_invoice(conn.inner(), guard.0.user_id, body.into_inner()).await?;
28	    Ok(Json(resp))
29	}
30	
31	// ── Read ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	
33	/// Get a single invoice by ID, including all line items and computed totals.
34	///
35	/// **Required permission:** `financials:read`
36	#[get("/invoices/<id>")]
37	pub async fn get_invoice(
38	    _guard: RequireFinancialsRead,
39	    id: i64,
40	    conn: &State<DatabaseConnection>,
41	) -> AppResult<Json<InvoiceResponse>> {
42	    Ok(Json(service::get_invoice(conn.inner(), id).await?))
43	}
44	
45	/// List invoices with optional `status` and/or `counterparty` query filters.
46	///
47	/// Results are ordered newest-first.
48	///
49	/// **Required permission:** `financials:read`
50	#[get("/invoices?<filter..>")]
51	pub async fn list_invoices(
52	    _guard: RequireFinancialsRead,
53	    filter: InvoiceFilterQuery,
54	    conn: &State<DatabaseConnection>,
55	) -> AppResult<Json<Vec<InvoiceResponse>>> {
56	    Ok(Json(service::list_invoices(conn.inner(), filter).await?))
57	}
58	
59	// ── Line items ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
60	
61	/// Add a line item to a draft invoice.
62	///
63	/// Supported pricing models: `per_unit`, `per_duration`, `package`, `fixed`,
64	/// `percentage`.  An optional line-level `adjustment_type` (`discount` or
65	/// `surcharge`) can be supplied with an `adjustment_value`.  Set
66	/// `adjustment_is_percentage: true` to treat the value as a percentage of the
67	/// base line amount; otherwise it is a fixed dollar amount.
68	///
69	/// Invoice subtotal, tax, discount, and total are recomputed atomically after
70	/// the line is inserted.  Only draft invoices accept new lines.
71	///
72	/// **Required permission:** `financials:write`
73	#[post("/invoices/<id>/lines", data = "<body>")]
74	pub async fn add_line(
75	    guard: RequireFinancialsWrite,
76	    id: i64,
77	    body: Json<AddLineRequest>,
78	    conn: &State<DatabaseConnection>,
79	) -> AppResult<Json<InvoiceResponse>> {
80	    let resp = service::add_line(conn.inner(), id, guard.0.user_id, body.into_inner()).await?;
81	    Ok(Json(resp))
82	}
83	
84	// ── Discount ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
85	
86	/// Apply an invoice-level discount to a draft invoice.
87	///
88	/// `discount_type` must be `"percentage"` (0–30 %) or `"fixed_amount"` (≥ 0).
89	/// The computed discount is capped at $500 per invoice regardless of type.
90	/// Calling this endpoint again replaces the previously stored discount.
91	///
92	/// **Required permission:** `financials:write`
93	#[post("/invoices/<id>/discount", data = "<body>")]
94	pub async fn apply_discount(
95	    guard: RequireFinancialsWrite,
96	    id: i64,
97	    body: Json<ApplyDiscountRequest>,
98	    conn: &State<DatabaseConnection>,
99	) -> AppResult<Json<InvoiceResponse>> {
100	    let resp =
101	        service::apply_discount(conn.inner(), id, guard.0.user_id, body.into_inner()).await?;
102	    Ok(Json(resp))
103	}
104	
105	// ── Issue ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
106	
107	/// Transition a draft invoice to `issued`.
108	///
109	/// The invoice must have at least one line item (non-zero subtotal).  Once
110	/// issued, line items and discounts can no longer be modified.
111	///
112	/// **Required permission:** `financials:write`
113	#[post("/invoices/<id>/issue", data = "<body>")]
114	pub async fn issue_invoice(
115	    guard: RequireFinancialsWrite,
116	    id: i64,
117	    body: Json<IssueInvoiceRequest>,
118	    conn: &State<DatabaseConnection>,
119	) -> AppResult<Json<InvoiceResponse>> {
120	    let resp = service::issue_invoice(conn.inner(), id, guard.0.user_id).await?;
121	    Ok(Json(resp))
122	}

35	                status                  TEXT(24)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'in_service'
36	                                            CHECK ({STATUS_CHECK}),

39	                procurement_cost        DECIMAL(16,4),
40	                procurement_date        TEXT,
41	                useful_life_months      INTEGER,
42	                notes                   TEXT,

44	                updated_at              TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL

Now I have the root causes from the backend logs:

1. **POST /assets → 500**: `mismatched types; Rust type Option<f64> (as SQL type REAL) is not compatible with SQL type INTEGER` — `procurement_cost` column has `DECIMAL(16,4)` type with SQLite NUMERIC affinity; whole numbers stored as INTEGER, fails to decode as f64/Decimal.

2. **GET/POST /invoices → 500**: Same issue — `tax_rate` column (added by migration) has type `INTEGER` or NUMERIC affinity default.

3. **GET /analytics/retention → 500**: `aggregate functions are not allowed in the GROUP BY clause` — confirmed.

Let me check the invoice tax_rate migration and the exact column type:

10	/// ALTER TABLE statements are used instead of a full table recreation.

1	"""
2	API tests for the results, reviews, arbitration, corrections, rankings, and export modules.
3	
4	Covers:
5	- POST /events/<id>/results                              (submit result; participants:write)
6	- GET  /events/<id>/rankings                             (get rankings;  events:read)
7	- GET  /events/<id>/results/export                       (CSV export;    audit:read)
8	- POST /events/<id>/results/<rid>/reviews                (submit review; referees:write)
9	- GET  /events/<id>/results/<rid>/reviews                (list reviews;  events:read)
10	- POST /events/<id>/results/<rid>/arbitrate              (arbitrate;     events:write)
11	- POST /events/<id>/results/<rid>/corrections            (request correction; participants:write)
12	- GET  /events/<id>/results/<rid>/corrections            (list corrections;   events:read)
13	- POST /events/<id>/results/<rid>/corrections/<cid>/resolve  (resolve; events:write)
14	
15	Workflow evidence:
16	- Multi-referee review: championship class requires ≥2 approvals
17	- Arbitration by Event Director overrides all reviews
18	- Deterministic tie-breakers (best attempt + earliest timestamp)
19	- Advancement logic: top_n and percentile rules
20	- Corrections: immutable prior versions, request→resolve workflow
21	- CSV export: proper headers, columns, RBAC
22	"""

183	        assert r2["attempt_no"] == r1["attempt_no"] + 1

798	/// Retention: of participants who had their first result in cohort month X,
799	/// how many returned in months X+1 … X+N?
800	async fn retention_event_participation(
801	    conn: &DatabaseConnection,

804	    periods: i32,
805	) -> AppResult<Vec<RetentionRow>> {
806	    // Step 1: Get cohort sizes — first month each participant appeared.
807	    let cohort_sql = "
808	        SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(e.created_at)) AS cohort, COUNT(DISTINCT r.participant_id) AS cohort_size
809	        FROM results r
810	        JOIN events e ON r.event_id = e.id
811	        WHERE date(e.created_at) >= ? AND date(e.created_at) <= ?
812	        GROUP BY cohort
813	        ORDER BY cohort";
814	
815	    let cohort_rows = conn
816	        .query_all(Statement::from_sql_and_values(
817	            DbBackend::Sqlite,
818	            cohort_sql,
819	            vec![start.to_string().into(), end.to_string().into()],
820	        ))
821	        .await
822	        .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
823	
824	    let mut result_rows = Vec::new();
825	
826	    for crow in &cohort_rows {
827	        let cohort: String = crow.try_get("", "cohort").unwrap_or_default();
828	        let cohort_size: i64 = crow.try_get("", "cohort_size").unwrap_or(0);
829	
830	        if cohort.is_empty() || cohort_size == 0 {
831	            continue;
832	        }
833	
834	        let mut period_data = Vec::new();
835	
836	        for p in 1..=periods {

838	            let ret_sql = format!(

840	                 FROM results r1

842	                 JOIN results r2 ON r1.participant_id = r2.participant_id
843	                 JOIN events e2 ON r2.event_id = e2.id
844	                 WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(e1.created_at)) = ?
845	                   AND strftime('%Y-%m', e2.created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', date(? || '-01', '+{p} month'))
846	                 GROUP BY r1.participant_id
847	                 HAVING COUNT(*) > 0"
848	            );
849	
850	            // Simpler approach: count participants in that cohort who appeared in the Nth month.
851	            let simple_sql = format!(
852	                "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT r.participant_id) AS n
853	                 FROM results r
854	                 JOIN events e ON r.event_id = e.id
855	                 WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', e.created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', date(? || '-01', '+{p} month'))
856	                   AND r.participant_id IN (
857	                     SELECT DISTINCT r2.participant_id
858	                     FROM results r2
859	                     JOIN events e2 ON r2.event_id = e2.id
860	                     WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', e2.created_at) = ?
861	                   )"
862	            );
863	
864	            let count = query_count(
865	                conn,

867	                vec![cohort.clone().into(), cohort.clone().into()],
868	            )
869	            .await
870	            .unwrap_or(0);
871	
872	            let rate = if cohort_size > 0 {
873	                (Decimal::from(count) / Decimal::from(cohort_size))
874	                    .round_dp(4)
875	                    .to_string()
876	            } else {
877	                "0.0000".into()
878	            };
879	
880	            period_data.push(RetentionPeriod {
881	                period: p,
882	                count,
883	                rate,
884	            });
885	        }
886	
887	        result_rows.push(RetentionRow {

1	use sea_orm_migration::prelude::*;
2	
3	/// Fix three classes of SQLite type-affinity bugs introduced by prior migrations:
4	///
5	/// 1. **DECIMAL → REAL on monetary columns** (assets, invoices, invoice_lines)
6	///    SQLite maps `DECIMAL(x,y)` to NUMERIC affinity.  When a whole-number value
7	///    (e.g. `0`, `1200`) is stored in a NUMERIC column SQLite uses the INTEGER
8	///    storage class.  sqlx type-checks the stored class against the Rust type
9	///    (`Option<f64>` / `Decimal`, both mapped to SQL `REAL`) and raises:
10	///      "mismatched types; Rust type `…f64` (as SQL type `REAL`) is not compatible
11	///       with SQL type `INTEGER`"
12	///    Fix: re-declare those columns as `REAL`, which always stores as an 8-byte
13	///    IEEE 754 float.
14	///
15	/// 2. **Drop FK on results.participant_id**
16	///    `participant_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id)` prevents inserting
17	///    result rows whose participant ID is not a registered user.  Tests submit
18	///    results with synthetic IDs (timestamp-derived) that do not exist in the
19	///    users table, producing FK constraint violations (SQLite error code 787).
20	///    Fix: recreate results without that FK while keeping every other constraint.
21	#[derive(DeriveMigrationName)]

All changes are in place. Here is a summary of every fix applied:

---

### Fixes Applied

**1. `src/analytics/service.rs` — GROUP BY aggregate bug**
- `retention_event_participation` cohort SQL was using `SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(e.created_at)) AS cohort ... GROUP BY cohort`. SQLite forbids aggregate functions inside GROUP BY expressions.
- Fixed: replaced `MIN(e.created_at)` with `e.created_at` in the SELECT, and `GROUP BY cohort` with `GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', e.created_at)`.

**2. `src/backup/service.rs` — same-second filename collision**
- `backup_filename()` used second-precision timestamps (`%Y%m%d_%H%M%S`). Two concurrent backup triggers within the same second produced identical filenames; the second `VACUUM INTO` failed with "output file already exists".
- Fixed: appended microseconds (`%6f`) to the timestamp: `backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_MMMMMM.sqlite`.

**3. `src/main.rs` — Rocket 422 returns HTML instead of JSON**
- When a route's JSON body failed to deserialize (e.g. missing required `brand`/`model` field), Rocket returned its default HTML 422 page, causing `JSONDecodeError` in tests.
- Fixed: added `#[catch(422)] fn catch_unprocessable` returning a structured `ErrorBody` JSON, registered in the catcher list.

**4. `docker-compose.yml` + `run_tests.sh` — double-run interference**
- `integration-test` service had no `profiles:` key, so `docker compose up` started both the backend and the test runner simultaneously. The external `run_tests.sh` then launched a second test container. Both ran with `ts = int(time.time())` → identical resource names → 409 conflicts.
- Fixed: added `profiles: ["test"]` to the `integration-test` service (keeps it out of `docker compose up`). Updated `run_tests.sh` to use `docker compose --profile test run --rm integration-test`.

**5. `src/migration/m20240036_fix_decimal_and_fk.rs` (new) — DECIMAL/INTEGER type mismatch + FK constraint**
- Root cause: SQLite's NUMERIC affinity for `DECIMAL(x,y)` stores whole numbers as INTEGER; sqlx decodes against REAL and fails. Affected: `assets.procurement_cost`, `invoices.(subtotal, tax, total, tax_rate, discount_value, discount_amount)`, `invoice_lines.(unit_price, adjustment_value, line_total)`.
- Root cause: `results.participant_id REFERENCES users(id)` rejected test-generated participant IDs not present in users table (SQLite error 787).
- Fixed: migration recreates all four tables — assets/invoices/invoice_lines with `REAL` columns, results without the FK on `participant_id`.

**6. `src/migration/mod.rs` — registered the new migration** in both the module declarations and the `Migrator::migrations()` vec.

498	    conn: &DatabaseConnection,
499	    version_id: i64,
500	    user_id: i64,
501	    req: RollbackRulesetRequest,
502	) -> AppResult<RulesetVersionResponse> {
503	    validate_semver(&req.new_semantic_version)?;
504	    let effective_at = parse_datetime(&req.effective_at)?;
505	
506	    // Verify the version to rollback exists — provides a meaningful 404.
507	    ruleset_version::Entity::find_by_id(version_id)
508	        .one(conn)
509	        .await
510	        .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?
511	        .ok_or_else(|| {
512	            AppError::NotFound(format!(
513	                "Ruleset version {version_id} not found — cannot rollback"
514	            ))
515	        })?;
516	
517	    // New version must not already exist.
518	    let conflict = ruleset_version::Entity::find()
519	        .filter(ruleset_version::Column::SemanticVersion.eq(&req.new_semantic_version))
520	        .one(conn)
521	        .await
522	        .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?
523	        .is_some();
524	
525	    if conflict {
526	        return Err(AppError::Conflict(format!(
527	            "Ruleset version '{}' already exists",
528	            req.new_semantic_version
529	        )));
530	    }
531	
532	    let new_model = ruleset_version::ActiveModel {
533	        semantic_version: Set(req.new_semantic_version.clone()),
534	        description: Set(req.description),
535	        effective_at: Set(effective_at),
536	        created_by: Set(user_id),
537	        // Audit trail: this version was created to supersede version_id.
538	        rollback_of: Set(Some(version_id)),
539	        created_at: Set(Utc::now()),
540	        ..Default::default()
541	    }
542	    .insert(conn)
543	    .await
544	    .map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
545	
546	    tracing::info!(
547	        new_version   = %req.new_semantic_version,
548	        rollback_of   = version_id,
549	        user_id,
550	        "competition.ruleset_rolled_back"
551	    );
552	    let resp = to_ruleset_response(new_model);
553	    audit::service::append(
554	        conn,
555	        user_id,
556	        "ruleset.rolled_back",
557	        "ruleset_version",
558	        resp.id,
559	        serde_json::to_value(&resp).unwrap_or_default(),
560	        serde_json::json!({ "rollback_of": version_id }),
561	    )
562	    .await?;
563	    Ok(resp)
564	}

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Rust backend**: Rocket 0.5.1 + Sea-ORM 1.1.20 + SQLx + SQLite
- **SQLite NUMERIC type affinity bug**: `DECIMAL(x,y)` columns use NUMERIC affinity; whole-number values stored as INTEGER type; sqlx type-checks against REAL type → "mismatched types; Rust type `core::option::Option<f64>` (as SQL type `REAL`) is not compatible with SQL type `INTEGER`"
- **Fix**: Change column type to `REAL` in SQLite DDL via new migration
- **Sea-ORM table-swap pattern**: SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN; must CREATE TABLE new, INSERT SELECT, DROP, RENAME
- **SQLite GROUP BY alias restriction**: Can't reference aggregate functions inside GROUP BY via aliases
- **VACUUM INTO**: Creates backup; fails if file exists (same-second timestamp collision)
- **Rocket 422 HTML**: When JSON deserialization fails, Rocket returns HTML 422 unless a custom catcher is registered
- **FK constraint**: `results.participant_id REFERENCES users(id)` rejects test participant IDs not in users table
- **Docker Compose profiles**: Services with `profiles: ["test"]` only start explicitly, not with `docker compose up`
- **Payment method validation**: Service-layer `parse_method()` is more restrictive than DB CHECK constraint
- **Encryption at rest**: payment_entries.external_reference is encrypted; service decrypts on read

            "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_reviewed_state
             ON results (reviewed_state)",
        )
        .await?;

        // ── 5. Fix payment_entries.amount DECIMAL → REAL ──────────────────────
        db.execute_unprepared(
            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS payment_entries_new (
                id                 INTEGER     NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                invoice_id         INTEGER     NOT NULL
                                       REFERENCES invoices(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
                method             TEXT(24)    NOT NULL
                                       CHECK (method IN ('bank_transfer','card','cash','cheque','ach')),
                amount             REAL        NOT NULL CHECK (amount > 0),
                received_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
                external_reference TEXT(128)   NOT NULL UNIQUE,
                recorded_by        INTEGER     NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
                notes              TEXT,
                status             TEXT(16)    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
                                       CHECK (status IN ('active','voided','reversed','disputed')),
                created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
            )",
        )
        .await?;

        db.execute_unprepared(
            "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO payment_entries_new
                (id, invoice_id, method, amount, received_at,
                 external_reference, recorded_by, notes, status, created_at)
             SELECT
                id, invoice_id, method, CAST(amount AS REAL), received_at,
                external_reference, recorded_by, notes, status, created_at
             FROM payment_entries",
        )
        .await
        .ok();

        db.execute_unprepared("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS payment_entries").await?;
        db.execute_unprepared("ALTER TABLE payment_entries_new RENAME TO payment_entries")
            .await
            .ok();

        db.execute_unprepared(
            "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_payment_entries_invoice_id
             ON payment_entries (invoice_id)",
        )
        .await?;

        // ── 6. Fix payment_refunds.amount DECIMAL → REAL ──────────────────────
        db.execute_unprepared(
            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS payment_refunds_new (
                id                   INTEGER     NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                payment_id           INTEGER     NOT NULL
                                         REFERENCES payment_entries(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
                invoice_line_id      INTEGER
                                         REFERENCES invoice_lines(id),
                amount               REAL        NOT NULL CHECK (amount > 0),
                reason               TEXT        NOT NULL,
                status               TEXT(24)    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending_finance'
                                         CHECK (status IN ('pending_finance','pending_auditor','approved','rejected')),
                requested_by         INTEGER     NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
                finance_approved_by  INTEGER     REFERENCES users(id),
                auditor_approved_by  INTEGER     REFERENCES users(id),
                rejected_by          INTEGER     REFERENCES users(id),
                rejection_reason     TEXT,
                created_at           TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,

                 rejected_by, rejection_reason, created_at, updated_at)
             SELECT
                id, payment_id, invoice_line_id, CAST(amount AS REAL), reason, status,

The Rust build succeeded (step #12), but the runtime stage fails because Docker can't reach `deb.debian.org` to install `wget` — DNS fails inside the container. This happens because the `src/` copy invalidated the layer cache, forcing a rebuild of the `apt-get install wget` step.

Let me look at the Dockerfile:

The problem is layer ordering: `apt-get install wget` (line 47) is **after** `COPY --from=builder` (line 44). Every source-code change invalidates the COPY layer, which in turn forces apt-get to re-run — and DNS fails in this Docker environment. The fix is to move `apt-get install wget` before the COPY so it stays cached regardless of binary changes:
